text: When you write code, it is normal to make mistakes. These mistakes, or bugs, can prevent your program from running correctly. The first step is identifying bugs. You need to find exactly where the problem is. A good approach is to be systematic: test one part of your code at a time, rather than guessing randomly. Using debugging tools, like breakpoints in your code editor, can help you pause the program and inspect variables. Another useful technique is logging. By adding print statements or writing messages to a log file, you can see what your program is doing step by step. With a clear, step-by-step method, you will solve bugs more quickly.
